trustBar.signal3: No cost if we don't collect

home.heroH1Line2: Protect your cash flow and reputation.
home.heroSubtitle: We recover your commercial accounts receivable up to $15K. You only pay when we collect.
home.problem1Title: Your cash flow takes the hit
home.problem1Body: You finished a $5,000 subcontracting job. The contractor promises to pay, then goes silent. You need that $5,000 for materials, payroll, and your next project.
home.problem2Body: You've spent months chasing a client over an accounting services invoice. Every ignored attempt takes time away from serving clients who actually pay on time.
home.step1Description: We schedule a brief consultation to evaluate your case and define the collection strategy.
home.step2Description: Send us your invoices, contracts, and any evidence related to the debt via email, WhatsApp, or text message.
home.step3Description: Webster handles the rest. We consult you on any debtor offers and ask before escalating the process.
home.processH2: A process designed to resolve your case.
home.processSubtitle: We only escalate when necessary, and only with your approval.
home.stage1Description: We assess your situation and decide together on the best approach, from a friendly reminder to a formal demand letter. We negotiate directly with the debtor and keep you informed. Most cases are resolved here.
home.mockupDebtorMessage: Hello, we received your communication. We will not pay. The products were defective.
home.mockupWebsterMessage: Thank you for responding to our email. We reviewed the documentation for this transaction. Attached is a signed receipt from your staff confirming the products were received in good condition. Do you have any evidence you can send us showing the products arrived defective?
home.stage2Description: If direct negotiation is not enough, we file a summary collection action in court to obtain a judgment in your favor.
home.stage3Description: If necessary, we evaluate and execute garnishment procedures or other legal mechanisms to enforce the judgment obtained.
home.why1Body: Give your business breathing room by collecting all the money scattered across your clients.
home.why2Body: Stop chasing clients to get paid.
home.why3Body: Impress your clients by showing your company has the best resources to operate.
home.why4Body: Your clients will be happy to pay. Surprise them with a firm but respectful attorney who makes paying easy.
home.aiPullQuote: The strength of a firm. The speed of technology.
home.aiPoint1: We use AI to draft legal documents, analyze your documentation, and coordinate every stage of the collection process.
home.aiPoint2: AI ensures consistency and precision in every action, eliminating the errors that make the traditional process expensive and slow.
home.aiPoint3: Every document is reviewed and signed by an attorney before it's sent.
home.pricingSubtitle: Fees that fit your needs. We help with commercial debts from $1,000 to $15,000.
home.pricingDisclaimer: Zero deposits or hourly billing. You only pay when we collect.
home.faq1A: No. Webster is a law firm, not a collection agency. We prioritize preserving your business relationship throughout the process before taking any serious legal action.
home.faq2Q: What's the minimum debt to work with Webster?
home.faq2A: Webster handles commercial debts from $1,000 to $15,000. If your debt falls outside this range, we'll let you know during the free consultation and guide you on your options.
home.faq3Q: How does the fee model work?
home.faq3A: Webster works on contingency fees. We only charge a percentage of what we recover for you. If we don't recover anything, you owe nothing.
home.faq4Q: How long does the collection process take?
home.faq4A: It depends on the case complexity and the debtor's response. Many pre-litigation efforts are resolved within the first two to four weeks. Cases requiring litigation can take several months.
home.faq5Q: What documents do I need to get started?
home.faq5A: Generally we need: the original invoice or contract, evidence of the debt (emails, purchase orders, delivery receipts), and any prior communication with the debtor. During the initial consultation, we'll tell you exactly what applies to your case.
home.faq6Q: Can I submit more than one invoice?
home.faq6A: Yes. Many businesses have multiple outstanding receivables. We evaluate each one and give you a complete strategy.
home.faq7Q: Will the debtor know I hired Webster?
home.faq7A: Yes. We communicate directly with the debtor on your behalf. All communication is professional, clear, and backed by an attorney.
home.faq8Q: Does Webster handle collections outside Puerto Rico?
home.faq8A: We currently focus on commercial debts under Puerto Rico law. If you have a debt connected to another jurisdiction, we can evaluate it during the free consultation.
home.faq9Q: What if the debtor doesn't have money to pay?
home.faq9A: During the initial consultation, we assess the viability of collection. If we determine the debtor lacks the ability to pay or that recovery is unlikely, we communicate that transparently before proceeding. We don't take cases that don't have a reasonable chance of success.

blog.subtitle: Legal updates, collection strategies, and commercial law news in Puerto Rico.
blog.featuredTitle: Five mistakes businesses make when collecting overdue invoices
blog.featuredExcerpt: Many businesses wait too long to act when a client doesn't pay. In this article, we explain the most common mistakes and how to avoid them to protect your cash flow.
blog.sidebarNewsletterSubtitle: Get articles and legal updates delivered straight to your inbox.
blog.post1Title: How to protect your receivables before it's too late
blog.post1Excerpt: Prevention is the best collection strategy. Learn how to structure your contracts and billing processes to minimize the risk of non-payment.
blog.post2Title: What to do when a client doesn't pay: A practical guide
blog.post2Excerpt: A step-by-step guide on the legal options available when a commercial client fails to pay an invoice.
blog.post3Title: Pre-litigation vs. litigation: When to escalate collection
blog.post3Excerpt: Not every case requires a lawsuit. We explain how to evaluate when out-of-court action is enough and when litigation is necessary.
blog.post4Title: The impact of uncollectible accounts on your business
blog.post4Excerpt: Overdue receivables affect more than your balance sheet. They affect your ability to operate, invest, and grow.
blog.post5Title: Commercial contracts: Clauses that protect your right to collect
blog.post5Excerpt: The right clauses in your contracts can be the difference between getting paid and absorbing the loss. These are the essentials.
blog.post6Title: Commercial collections in Puerto Rico: What every business owner should know
blog.post6Excerpt: An introduction to the legal framework for commercial collections in Puerto Rico, including prescriptive periods, jurisdiction, and available remedies.

contadores.label: For Accountants · Webster Legal
contadores.h1: Become the accountant who solves the client's problems.
contadores.subtitle: When a client's receivables haven't moved in months, refer them to us. Webster recovers unpaid B2B invoices with real legal backing.
contadores.sectionTitle: A collection agency is not the same as a law firm.
contadores.bullet1Bold: An attorney letter changes the conversation.
contadores.bullet1Rest: Debtors cooperate with attorneys because they want to avoid bigger legal problems.
contadores.bullet2Rest: Webster looks after your client's interests with higher ethical duties than a collection agency.
contadores.bullet3Bold: Webster has multiple tools at its disposal.
contadores.bullet3Rest: Webster can use the court system if the debtor refuses to pay.
contadores.howTitle: Simple for you. Simple for your client.
contadores.step1Description: Tell them to call or fill out the form.
contadores.step2Description: Clear answer: what we can do, what the strategy is, when to expect results.
contadores.step3Description: Most cases are resolved before reaching court, with both parties satisfied.
contadores.ctaFinalText: Next time a client mentions or you identify an unpaid invoice, you know who to recommend.

sobreNosotros.intro: Webster is a law firm that uses AI to deliver faster, more precise legal services. We automate the processes that make legal work usually expensive so our clients receive high-quality service at lower cost.
sobreNosotros.founderBio: Graduated from the U.P.R. School of Law with high honors. Adrián Jiménez is a licensed attorney in Puerto Rico with six years of experience in federal and state commercial litigation. He served as a law clerk at the U.S. District Court for the District of Puerto Rico under Judges Gustavo Gelpí and Silvia Carreño Coll. He also worked at the law firms DLA Piper (PR) and Pietrantoni Méndez & Álvarez.
